title: senna surattensis subsp. sulfurea
intro: Senna surattensis subsp. sulfurea, also known as the golden cassia or pineline, is a plant species belonging to the legume family and is native to parts of Asia. The plant contains anthraquinone glycosides, which are responsible for its medicinal properties, including its laxative and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of Senna surattensis subsp. sulfurea include its ability to aid digestion, reduce fever, and relieve symptoms of constipation, diarrhea, and menstrual cramps. Senna surattensis subsp. sulfurea is commonly prepared as a tea, capsule, or tincture, and is traditionally used in herbal medicine to promote overall well-being and alleviate various health issues.
